channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bishop Criticizes Political Hypocrisy and Calls for National Unity in Tanzania
item.description: Bishop Dr Glorius Shoo, a retired Tanzania Assembly of God bishop, criticizes the nation's culture of deception and threats, arguing that these practices have eroded citizens' hope and created a dange...

item.title: Edward Stourton, BBC Broadcaster and Journalist, Dies at 68 After Battle with Prostate Cancer
item.description: Edward Stourton, a respected BBC broadcaster and journalist known for his work on Radio 4's Today programme, has passed away at the age of 68. Diagnosed with prostate cancer in 2014, he died surrounde...

item.title: US Soldier Killed in Iraq as One Nation Surges in NSW Polls; Antisemitism and Global Tensions Rise
item.description: The article details multiple breaking news stories from around the world. A US service member was killed in Iraq, escalating tensions in the region. Meanwhile, Australia's One Nation party saw a signi...

item.title: South Africa's football development gap: Lessons from Europe's success
item.description: The article analyzes why African nations, including South Africa, struggle to compete with European football powers in the World Cup. It highlights how European countries prioritize youth development,...

item.title: Celebrities Born on the Same Date Highlighted by ABS-CBN Studios
item.description: The article by ABS-CBN Studios reveals that several celebrities share the same birthday, offering a unique insight into their lives. Published on July 20, 2026, the piece highlights the significance o...

item.title: Vehicle with Afghan families swept away by flash flood in Khyber, three bodies recovered
item.description: A vehicle carrying Afghan families returning to their homeland was swept away by a flash flood in the Khyber district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa on Monday. Rescue 1122 confirmed the recovery of three bodie...

item.title: Foreign Affairs PS Korir Denies Running for 2027 Election
item.description: Principal Secretary for Foreign Affairs, Abraham Sing’Oei Korir, has officially denied rumors linking him to a 2027 elective position. The denial comes after a resurfaced 2022 United Democratic Allian...

item.title: Okonkwo Accuses APC of Using Propaganda to Defeat Jonathan in 2015 Election
item.description: Kenneth Okonkwo, a chieftain of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), claims the All Progressives Congress (APC) secured victory in Nigeria's 2015 election through sustained propaganda. He alleges th...

item.title: Public Opposition to AI Data Centres in Canada Rises Amid Environmental Concerns
item.description: The article explores growing resistance to AI data centre projects in Canada, highlighting community concerns about environmental impacts and resource use. While the Canadian government supports these...

item.title: U.S.-Iran Tensions Escalate Amid Airstrikes and Retaliatory Attacks
item.description: The article details the escalating conflict between the United States and Iran, with the U.S. conducting a new round of airstrikes targeting Iran after the death of an American service member in Iraq....

item.title: Trump's Qatari-Gifted Air Force One Faces Security Upgrades Amid Concerns
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has stated that the Qatari-donated Air Force One, which has been retrofitted with new security features, will undergo further upgrades to address concerns about its safety ...

item.title: Hunter Biden Criticizes Trump and MAGA Trolls in Defamation Victory
item.description: The article details Hunter Biden's ongoing battle against MAGA trolls and Donald Trump, highlighting his seven-year sobriety and legal victories. It discusses his defamation lawsuit against Patrick By...

item.title: Tripura DGP Anurag Dhankar's Death Under Investigation
item.description: Tripura Director General of Police (DGP) Anurag Dhankar was found dead at his office in the state police headquarters in Agartala. Officials confirmed the incident, with Chief Minister Manik Saha and ...

item.title: Former Chief Justice David Maraga Announces 2027 Presidential Campaign Leadership Team

item.title: Tanzania's Infrastructure Projects Boost Economic Growth
item.description: The article highlights key developments in Tanzania's economic and infrastructure sectors. It discusses the government's decision to align market fees with lease agreements, aiming to create a fairer ...

item.title: Senate impeachment court allows subpoena of Sara Duterte’s financial records for corruption probe
item.description: Former Senator Franklin Drilon asserted that Vice President Sara Duterte's bank and tax records are exempt from confidentiality laws due to her ongoing impeachment trial. The Senate impeachment court ...

item.title: Abduction and rape of foreign women: Three suspects arrested in Lahore
item.description: Two foreign women were abducted and subjected to sexual violence by suspects in Lahore, leading to the arrest of three individuals. The victims, after being held hostage for ransom, managed to escape ...

item.title: Strengthening Canada's Pharmaceutical Supply Chain Through Collaboration
item.description: The article highlights the complexity of Canada's pharmaceutical supply chain and the critical need for collaboration among stakeholders to ensure reliable access to medicines. Angelique Berg, preside...

item.title: Affidavit challenges Inspector General's suspension and raises questions about intelligence oversight
item.description: An affidavit filed by suspended Inspector General of Intelligence Imtiaz Fazel in the High Court raises serious allegations about the handling of intelligence oversight in South Africa. Fazel is chall...

item.title: Atiku demands transparency over massive budget allocation surge
item.description: Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar has accused President Bola Tinubu of hiding trillions of naira in unaccountable spending through the Service-Wide Vote (SWV), which saw a 1,918% surge from N638 bi...
